lead: Cody, students and teachers build it together — generation after generation.

notProduct.lead: Craft Codex is not a finished tool you buy. It is a living, growing universe of craft knowledge — and it belongs to everyone who builds it.
notProduct.sub: It starts small: with the dovetails in the cabinetmaker's shop. But every trade is its own world in this universe — metalworking, electrical work, mechanical engineering, every craft and every technical discipline. The universe keeps expanding, world by world, master by master, generation by generation.

why.title: With every master who goes, a <hl>lifetime of knowledge</hl> dies.
why.body: Until now, that loss had no way back. Not anymore: the old master's knowledge can be captured, checked and passed on — not as a dead textbook, but as a patient digital master who never tires and answers in the apprentice's own language. The craft isn't preserved like a museum piece. It lives on and grows into the AI age.

how.kicker: How it gets built — the community is the master builder
how.title: No one person builds it. Cody turns the community's words into<hl> living tools</hl>.

roles.what: is the source. His knowledge, his eye, his feel for the material — that is the raw stock.
guides and organizes. He turns experience into curriculum.
builds. He describes the craft in his own words, and Cody shapes them into an interactive tool — voice, VR, right at the workpiece.
is the invisible engine. He replaces no one. He is the loom the community weaves on — you never see him, you only see what takes shape.

how.note1: Building is the curriculum. As students expand the universe, they learn a new discipline — applied AI: they learn the craft, and they learn to build the AI for it.
how.note2: Before a tool teaches anyone, a master signs it off. The digital master invents nothing — every answer stands on verified knowledge from a real source.

flywheel.chain: more students build → more master knowledge gets captured → better tools → more trades → more students who see the craft as a future again → and they build even more.
flywheel.body: No single person is the bottleneck. Cody scales the building, the community scales the knowledge — from the cabinetmaker to the metalworker, without any one person having to build every tool alone.

items.what: his life's work isn't lost — it carries on. And the machine takes over the endlessly repeated work: time back for what only a human can do.
a new curriculum, and students who can do something that didn't exist before.
learns from a patient master around the clock — and learns to build AI at the same time. Whoever masters the craft and the AI to go with it is irreplaceable.
becomes the pioneer of a new discipline and keeps its young people in the valley instead of losing them.
a craft knowledge commons that stays open. The output of this layer is free. Made in Europe, and it stays in Europe.

engine.body: Cody himself stays hidden. Nobody sees him — the community only sees what takes shape: the growing universe. The engine is the secret, the output is the gift. That way the universe can be generously open without ever giving away what it was woven with.

horizon.body: In ten, in twenty years, the universe carries the collected knowledge of countless masters from every trade — continuously expanded by each new generation, freely available, taught by tireless digital masters. And alongside it, the irreplaceable remains: the hand, the eye, the judgment of a human being.
horizon.punch: The old master's knowledge no longer dies with him. It lives on — and every generation builds it bigger.
horizon.tagline: That is the Craft Codex universe.
